reasoning: The current value (as of 11-07) is 0.37, which is a local spike relative to the 0.30-0.35 range seen from July to October 2025. Given that corporate spreads are near historic lows, there is significant asymmetry in the long term (higher probability of a large upward spike than a large drop, since the floor is around 0.30). However, in the very short term (1 week), local mean reversion back to the 0.33-0.35 range is highly probable, making an increase above 0.37 unlikely. Over intermediate (1 month) and longer (3 months) horizons, the probability of spreads rising above 0.37 increases as the chance of normal spread widening or macroeconomic shocks offsets the short-term mean reversion. Therefore, the probabilities gently increase toward ~0.50 as the horizon extends.
